$comment: Schema for RF parser JSON output. Two formats share all top-level scalars and waveforms; they differ only in the timeline field.

WaveformEntry.description: One distinct RF pulse shape. Multiple timeline pulse events may reference the same waveform_id.

waveform_id.description: 0-based index; referenced by timeline pulse events.

n_points.description: Length of every sample array in this entry (= top-level n_points).

max_twos_complement.description: 2^(n_bits-1) - 1; the integer that maps to normalised amplitude 1.0.

waveform_raw.description: Magnitude at each sample in Hz; peak equals rf_amplitude_hz.

waveform_twos_complement.description: Signed DAC codes; peak maps to max_twos_complement.

phase_rad.description: Instantaneous phase at each sample in radians.

sampling_time_s.description: Duration of one output sample = rf_duration_s / n_points.

rf_duration_s.description: Total RF pulse duration in seconds.

rf_amplitude_hz.description: Peak amplitude in Hz (= maximum of waveform_raw).

rf_raster_time_s.description: Native RF raster interval from the .seq file before resampling.

n_samples_original.description: Native sample count at rf_raster_time_s before resampling to n_points.

GapEvent.description: A silent interval; no RF is transmitted.

PulseEvent.description: An RF transmission event using a waveform from the waveforms array.

pulse_type.description: Informational; mirrors the referenced waveform entry.

gain.description: Multi-channel: scale applied to waveform_normalized; effective peak = gain * rf_amplitude_hz.

phase.description: Multi-channel: additional phase (rad) added to phase_rad at playback time.

TimelineEvent.description: One entry in a timeline array: either a gap or a pulse, discriminated by the 'type' field.

SingleChannelSequence.description: Output of rf_waveform_parser.py or the TSE generator. timeline is a flat ordered array covering the active RF window (tail gap excluded).

tr_duration_s.description: Full TR period in seconds, including the tail gap.

initial_delay_s.description: Time from TR start to first RF waveform start (s); equals the first gap event duration.

tail_delay_s.description: Time from last RF waveform end to TR end (s); not a timeline event.

n_rf_pulses.description: Total RF pulse events in timeline (excitation + refocusing combined).

n_unique_waveforms.description: Number of entries in the waveforms array.

n_points.description: Resampled output length applied to every waveform, e.g. 4096.

waveforms.description: Distinct RF pulse shapes, indexed by waveform_id.

timeline.description: Ordered events from first gap through last pulse. Tail gap is excluded (see tail_delay_s).

MultiChannelSequence.description: Output of multichannel_json_parser.py. timeline is an object keyed by zero-based channel index strings ("0"…"N-1").

tr_duration_s.description: Full TR period in seconds, including the tail gap.

initial_delay_s.description: Time from TR start to first RF waveform start (s).

tail_delay_s.description: Time from last RF waveform end to TR end (s); not a timeline event.

n_rf_pulses.description: Total RF pulse events per channel.

n_unique_waveforms.description: Number of entries in the waveforms array.

n_points.description: Resampled output length applied to every waveform.

waveforms.description: Distinct RF pulse shapes, indexed by waveform_id.

timeline.description: Keys are zero-based channel indices as strings ("0"…"N-1"). All channels have equal-length event arrays; only gain and phase differ.

description: RF parser sequence JSON — either single-channel or multi-channel.
